resolved_requirements.core_objective: hit the ball back and forth
resolved_requirements.lose_condition: allow the ball to pass your paddle
resolved_requirements.sound: simple sound effects for ball hits

plan.game_concept: A simple 2D game where the player controls a spaceship that must dodge incoming asteroids to survive as long as possible.
plan.framework_rationale: Simple mechanics achievable with Canvas API; fewer dependencies = more reliable generation

core_mechanics.name: Dodge Asteroids
core_mechanics.description: The player moves the spaceship left and right to avoid colliding with falling asteroids.

core_mechanics.name: Score Points
core_mechanics.description: The player earns points for every second survived.

game_loop.init_description: Initialize game variables, set up canvas, and create initial entities.
game_loop.update_description: Update the position of the player and asteroids, check for collisions, and update the score.
game_loop.render_description: Clear the canvas and redraw the player and asteroids.

entities.name: Player Ship
entities.description: The player's spaceship that can move left and right.

entities.name: Asteroid
entities.description: Falling obstacles that the player must avoid.

plan.css_spec: #gameCanvas { background: black; display: block; margin: 0 auto; }
plan.js_architecture: Single file containing all game logic, including initialization, game loop, entity management, and collision detection.
acceptance_checks: Player can move left and right using arrow keys.
Asteroids spawn at random positions and fall downwards.
Game ends when the player collides with an asteroid.
Score increases by 1 point for every second survived.

game_files.index.html:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Space Dodger</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
</head>
<body>
    <canvas id="gameCanvas" width="800" height="600"></canvas>
    <script src="game.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

findings.description: No score/points variable found in AST variable declarations
Entity 'Player Ship' from plan not found in code
Asteroids are not being spawned at random positions; they are initialized at (0, 0) and only move downwards.
findings.suggested_fix: Modify the spawnAsteroid function to set the x position of the asteroid to a random value within the canvas width.

findings.description: Asteroids do not have a defined color property, leading to them not being rendered correctly.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a color property to the asteroid object when it is created in the spawnAsteroid function.

findings.description: The game does not implement a game-over state display or restart prompt for the player.
findings.suggested_fix: Implement a game-over screen that displays the final score and prompts the player to restart the game.

findings.description: The player starts at the bottom of the canvas, which may not be visually appealing or intuitive.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider starting the player ship at a higher y-coordinate to provide a better visual experience.

findings.description: Asteroids increase in speed over time, but the initial speed may be too slow, making the game too easy.
findings.suggested_fix: Adjust the initial speed of the asteroids to provide a more challenging experience from the start.

checks.name: acceptance_criterion_0
checks.details: [Manual check required] Player can move left and right using arrow keys.

checks.name: acceptance_criterion_1
checks.details: [Manual check required] Asteroids spawn at random positions and fall downwards.

checks.name: acceptance_criterion_2
checks.details: [Manual check required] Game ends when the player collides with an asteroid.

checks.name: acceptance_criterion_3
checks.details: [Manual check required] Score increases by 1 point for every second survived.

repair_instructions: [MAJOR] Asteroids are not being spawned at random positions as required by the game plan. in game.js
[MAJOR] Asteroids do not have a defined color property, leading to them not being rendered correctly. in game.js
[MAJOR] The player starts at a fixed position (x: 400, y: 550) which may not be visible if the canvas height is adjusted or if the game is played on smaller screens. in game.js
[MAJOR] Asteroids spawn at a fixed interval without increasing speed over time, which does not comply with the difficulty curve specified in the game plan. in game.js
